knowledge_base.tags: Ventricular septal rupture, Myocardial infarction complications, Left anterior descending artery, Oxygen step-up, Right heart catheterization, Mitral regurgitation
knowledge_base.objective: Ventricular septal rupture is a mechanical complication of myocardial infarction occurring 3 to 5 days post-MI (typically following LAD occlusion) that presents with sudden hemodynamic collapse, a harsh holosystolic murmur at the left sternal border with a thrill, and an oxygen saturation step-up from the right atrium to the right ventricle.
